content.problemStatement:
Hulk has a set of distinct points PPP in the 2D plane. His signature move is HulkHulkHulk SmashSmashSmash. He performed HulkHulkHulk SmashSmashSmash on the set PPP which caused the points to change, and so he obtained a new set of points P‚Ä≤P'P‚Ä≤.

More formally, HulkHulkHulk SmashSmashSmash makes the new set P‚Ä≤P'P‚Ä≤ in the following way:


Initially, let P‚Ä≤P'P‚Ä≤ be empty.
For every PiP_iPi‚Äã and PjP_jPj‚Äã such that 1‚â§i<j‚â§N1 \le i \lt j \le N1‚â§i<j‚â§N , add the mid point of PiP_iPi‚Äã and PjP_jPj‚Äã to the set P‚Ä≤P'P‚Ä≤ (only if it didn't exist in it already).
So, P‚Ä≤P'P‚Ä≤ is the set of midpoints of line segments connecting pairs of distinct points of PPP.
Note that points from PPP do not necessarily have to be in P‚Ä≤P'P‚Ä≤. See the samples below for a graphical example.

Find the number of points of P‚Ä≤P'P‚Ä≤ which lie on the convex hull of P‚Ä≤P'P‚Ä≤. A point is said to lie on the convex hull if the shape of the convex hull is changed when this point is removed.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, denoting the size of PPP.
The next NNN lines contain two space-separated integers each, where the iii-th line contains Pi.x,Pi.yP_i.x,P_i.yPi‚Äã.x,Pi‚Äã.y denoting the x-coordinate and y-coordinate of the iii-th point.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the number of points of P‚Ä≤P'P‚Ä≤ which lie on its convex hull.
constraints: The convex hull of the input set PPP has positive area
Sum of NNN over all tests cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105
Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10310^3103

Note that by our definition, points (1,4)(1, 4)(1,4) and (3,4)(3, 4)(3,4) are not considered to lie on the convex hull of P‚Ä≤P'P‚Ä≤, because removing one of them does not change the shape of the hull.
